[[Category:qq]][[sn:Oigami Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oigami Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 老神温泉 |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |コメント = 夏の老神温泉郷（2016年7月撮影） |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[沼田市]][[利根村|利根町]] |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 40 |緯度秒 = 16 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 12 |経度秒 = 35 |交通アクセス = [[上毛高原駅]]より[[路線バス|バス]]で70分
[[沼田駅]]よりバスで50分
[[沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|沼田IC]]より[[自動車|車]]で30分{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=老神温泉旅館組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |泉質 = （代表的なもの）[[単純温泉]]{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/senshitsu.html |title=温泉利用状況 |publisher=老神温泉旅館組合 |accessdate=2022-04-06 }} |泉温 = （代表的なもの）61.5 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 13 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = 165,355（2019年度推計入込数）{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=沼田市統計書（令和2年度版） |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [http://www.oigami.net/ 老神温泉旅館組合] |特記事項 = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|秋の老神温泉郷（2011年11月撮影）]] '''老神温泉'''（おいがみおんせん）は、[[群馬県]][[沼田市]][[利根村|利根町]]にある[[温泉]]。 == 泉質 == 老神温泉旅館組合公式ウェブサイトによると、老神温泉には8つの[[源泉]]がある。代表的な源泉の泉質は下記の通り。 * 泉質：[[単純温泉]] * 泉温：[[セルシウス度|摂氏]]61.5度 * 効能：[[アトピー性皮膚炎|乾燥性皮膚炎（アトピー）]]、[[関節リウマチ|慢性関節リューマチ]]、[[筋肉痛]]、[[神経痛]]、[[神経炎症|神経炎]]、[[創傷]]、[[蕁麻疹|じん麻疹]]など 8つの源泉を一覧に示す。各[[温泉宿]]ごとに1本ないし複数本の源泉が引湯されている。 * 老神1号泉 * 老神3号泉 * 老神5号泉 * 老神7号泉 * 老神8号泉 * 老神10号泉 * 観音薬湯 * 若乃湯3号泉 かつては15本の源泉があり、1150[[リットル]]毎分の湧出量があった{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 == 温泉街 == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|沼田市利根観光会館（沼田市利根町老神607番地1）
観光および地場産業の振興のため設置された施設{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}。582席の大ホールや、会議室を備える{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}。非常時は[[指定緊急避難場所]]・[[指定避難所]]としての役割も担う{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=沼田市 |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}。]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|川原に残る遺構
川原の岩壺から温泉が湧き出ており、増水時は浴槽が水没していたという{{Sfn |群馬県 |1934 | p=355 }}。]] 利根町老神・利根町穴原・利根町大楊にまたがる{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=老神温泉旅館組合 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }}。[[片品川]]が作った深い[[渓谷]]に沿って[[温泉街]]が広がっている。1935年（昭和10年）古くから[[皮膚病]]に効く[[湯治場]]として知られ、[[大正]]時代には[[若山牧水]]が当地を訪れている。[[1935]]（昭和10年）、老神温泉・穴原温泉・大楊温泉の旅館が組合を結成し、「老神[[温泉郷]]」と命名。[[1959]]（昭和34年）には[[薗原ダム]]の建設に伴い、4軒が対岸の高台に移転し、1軒が取り壊しに遭った{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 宿泊施設は[[明治]]時代初期に1軒・浴場1であったものが、[[昭和]]初期になると十数軒、最盛期の[[1983]]（昭和58年）には22軒を数えた{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。老神温泉旅館組合公式ウェブサイトによると、2022年時点での旅館数は13軒である。 [[金精トンネル]]や[[関越自動車道]]の開通により、[[尾瀬]]や[[日光市|日光]]への観光の拠点としての機能も担うようになった。毎年10月が入込のピークである{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 == イベント == * 2月：老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 2月 - 3月：老神温泉びっくりひな飾り{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 4月 - 11月：老神温泉朝市{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 5月：老神温泉大蛇まつり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 6月：老神温泉ポピーまつり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 8月：とねふるさと風のまつり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 8月：老神温泉納涼花火大会{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 9月：老神温泉そば祭り{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === 地名の由来 === 「老神」の地名の由来として、『[[角川日本地名大辞典]]』「老神」・「老神温泉」の項には次のような説が紹介されている{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 * [[赤城山]]の[[神]]と[[男体山|二荒山]]の神との争いで、赤城山の神が当地に敗走して来たこと（追神）から。あるいは、逆に赤城山の神が二荒山の神を追い返したこと（追神）から。 * 赤城山の神による開湯伝説（「湯の神」の転訛）から。 * 「お湯の上（かみ）」の転訛。 * 「老神」の表記は[[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]]年間に[[沼田城]]城主夫人が追神を訪れ、老い行く我が身とかけて「老が身」としたことから。 === 開湯伝説・神域 === 伝承によると赤城山の神と二荒山の神との間で領地を巡う争いがあり、やがて劣勢となって当地まで撤退を余儀なくされた赤城山の神によって温泉が開かれたとされる。赤城山の神が[[弓]]（あるいは[[矢]]）を立てた場所から温泉が湧出し、傷を癒やした赤城山の神は再び二荒山の神に挑み、打ち倒したという。[[古墳時代]]、「[[毛野|毛野国]]」が2分され、現在の群馬県（[[上野国]]）と[[栃木県]]（[[下野国]]）となる過程で起こった[[領土問題|国境争い]]から生まれた話とされる{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146 }}。 [[テレビアニメ]]『[[まんが日本昔ばなし]]』に見られるように、一般的には赤城山の神は大[[ムカデ]]、二荒山の神は[[ヘビ|大蛇]]とされる。しかし、老神温泉では赤城山の神が大蛇、二荒山の神が大ムカデと逆転して伝えられている{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}。これに関しては[[藤原秀郷]]のムカデ退治の話から、勝者を大蛇に、敗者を大ムカデに見立てるものだとすれば、老神温泉では赤城山の神が勝利したとされることから、勝利した赤城山の神が大蛇で、敗退した二荒山の神が大ムカデと言われるようになったとする研究がある{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147 }}。 因みに老神温泉に流れる片品川を挟んだ東側に二荒山神社、西側に赤城神社がある。 '''歌''' 老神おんど 老神は、いで湯町 == 交通アクセス == ;[[公共交通機関]] :[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[上越新幹線]]・[[上毛高原駅]]より[[路線バス]]（[[関越交通]]）で約70分間。 :JR[[上越線]]・[[沼田駅]]より路線バス（関越交通）で約50分間。 :{{要出典範囲|date=2021-04|一部のホテル・旅館では、上毛高原駅・沼田駅～老神温泉バス停間で、無料送迎[[バス (車両)|バス]]『じゃおう号』を共同運行している。対象は宿泊予約済みの客限定で、前日までに予約が必要。宿泊施設によっては同バスに参加していないところもある}}。 ;[[自家用自動車]] :[[関越自動車道]]・[[沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|沼田インターチェンジ]]より[[国道120号]]を約30分間。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1988 [[沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|沼田IC]]より[[自動車|車]]で30分{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=老神温泉旅館組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |泉質 = （代表的なもの）[[単純温泉]]{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/senshitsu.html |title=温泉利用状況 |publisher=老神温泉旅館組合 |accessdate=2022-04-06 }} |泉温 = （代表的なもの）61.5 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 13 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = 165,355（2019年度推計入込数）{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=沼田市統計書（令和2年度版） |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [http://www.oigami.net/ 老神温泉旅館組合] |特記事項 = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|秋の老神温泉郷（2011年11月撮影）]] '''老神温泉'''（おいがみおんせん）は、[[群馬県]][[沼田市]][[利根村|利根町]]にある[[温泉]]。 == 泉質 == 老神温泉旅館組合公式ウェブサイトによると、老神温泉には8つの[[源泉]]がある。代表的な源泉の泉質は下記の通り。 * 泉質：[[単純温泉]] * 泉温：[[セルシウス度|摂氏]]61.5度 * 効能：[[アトピー性皮膚炎|乾燥性皮膚炎（アトピー）]]、[[関節リウマチ|慢性関節リューマチ]]、[[筋肉痛]]、[[神経痛]]、[[神経炎症|神経炎]]、[[創傷]]、[[蕁麻疹|じん麻疹]]など 8つの源泉を一覧に示す。各[[温泉宿]]ごとに1本ないし複数本の源泉が引湯されている。 * 老神1号泉 * 老神3号泉 * 老神5号泉 * 老神7号泉 * 老神8号泉 * 老神10号泉 * 観音薬湯 * 若乃湯3号泉 かつては15本の源泉があり、1150[[リットル]]毎分の湧出量があった{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 == 温泉街 == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|沼田市利根観光会館（沼田市利根町老神607番地1）
観光および地場産業の振興のため設置された施設{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}。582席の大ホールや、会議室を備える{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}。非常時は[[指定緊急避難場所]]・[[指定避難所]]としての役割も担う{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=沼田市 |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}。]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|川原に残る遺構
川原の岩壺から温泉が湧き出ており、増水時は浴槽が水没していたという{{Sfn |群馬県 |1934 | p=355 }}。]] 利根町老神・利根町穴原・利根町大楊にまたがる{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=老神温泉旅館組合 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }}。[[片品川]]が作った深い[[渓谷]]に沿って[[温泉街]]が広がっている。1935年（昭和10年）古くから[[皮膚病]]に効く[[湯治場]]として知られ、[[大正]]時代には[[若山牧水]]が当地を訪れている。[[1935]]（昭和10年）、老神温泉・穴原温泉・大楊温泉の旅館が組合を結成し、「老神[[温泉郷]]」と命名。[[1959]]（昭和34年）には[[薗原ダム]]の建設に伴い、4軒が対岸の高台に移転し、1軒が取り壊しに遭った{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 宿泊施設は[[明治]]時代初期に1軒・浴場1であったものが、[[昭和]]初期になると十数軒、最盛期の[[1983]]（昭和58年）には22軒を数えた{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。老神温泉旅館組合公式ウェブサイトによると、2022年時点での旅館数は13軒である。 [[金精トンネル]]や[[関越自動車道]]の開通により、[[尾瀬]]や[[日光市|日光]]への観光の拠点としての機能も担うようになった。毎年10月が入込のピークである{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 == イベント == * 2月：老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 2月 - 3月：老神温泉びっくりひな飾り{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 4月 - 11月：老神温泉朝市{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 5月：老神温泉大蛇まつり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 6月：老神温泉ポピーまつり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 8月：とねふるさと風のまつり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 8月：老神温泉納涼花火大会{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 9月：老神温泉そば祭り{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === 地名の由来 === 「老神」の地名の由来として、『[[角川日本地名大辞典]]』「老神」・「老神温泉」の項には次のような説が紹介されている{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 * [[赤城山]]の[[神]]と[[男体山|二荒山]]の神との争いで、赤城山の神が当地に敗走して来たこと（追神）から。あるいは、逆に赤城山の神が二荒山の神を追い返したこと（追神）から。 * 赤城山の神による開湯伝説（「湯の神」の転訛）から。 * 「お湯の上（かみ）」の転訛。 * 「老神」の表記は[[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]]年間に[[沼田城]]城主夫人が追神を訪れ、老い行く我が身とかけて「老が身」としたことから。 === 開湯伝説・神域 === 伝承によると赤城山の神と二荒山の神との間で領地を巡う争いがあり、やがて劣勢となって当地まで撤退を余儀なくされた赤城山の神によって温泉が開かれたとされる。赤城山の神が[[弓]]（あるいは[[矢]]）を立てた場所から温泉が湧出し、傷を癒やした赤城山の神は再び二荒山の神に挑み、打ち倒したという。[[古墳時代]]、「[[毛野|毛野国]]」が2分され、現在の群馬県（[[上野国]]）と[[栃木県]]（[[下野国]]）となる過程で起こった[[領土問題|国境争い]]から生まれた話とされる{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146 }}。 [[テレビアニメ]]『[[まんが日本昔ばなし]]』に見られるように、一般的には赤城山の神は大[[ムカデ]]、二荒山の神は[[ヘビ|大蛇]]とされる。しかし、老神温泉では赤城山の神が大蛇、二荒山の神が大ムカデと逆転して伝えられている{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}。これに関しては[[藤原秀郷]]のムカデ退治の話から、勝者を大蛇に、敗者を大ムカデに見立てるものだとすれば、老神温泉では赤城山の神が勝利したとされることから、勝利した赤城山の神が大蛇で、敗退した二荒山の神が大ムカデと言われるようになったとする研究がある{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147 }}。 因みに老神温泉に流れる片品川を挟んだ東側に二荒山神社、西側に赤城神社がある。 '''歌''' 老神おんど 老神は、いで湯町 == 交通アクセス == ;[[公共交通機関]] :[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[上越新幹線]]・[[上毛高原駅]]より[[路線バス]]（[[関越交通]]）で約70分間。 :JR[[上越線]]・[[沼田駅]]より路線バス（関越交通）で約50分間。 :{{要出典範囲|date=2021-04|一部のホテル・旅館では、上毛高原駅・沼田駅～老神温泉バス停間で、無料送迎[[バス (車両)|バス]]『じゃおう号』を共同運行している。対象は宿泊予約済みの客限定で、前日までに予約が必要。宿泊施設によっては同バスに参加していないところもある}}。 ;[[自家用自動車]] :[[関越自動車道]]・[[沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|沼田インターチェンジ]]より[[国道120号]]を約30分間。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1988 観光および地場産業の振興のため設置された施設{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}。582席の大ホールや、会議室を備える{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}。非常時は[[指定緊急避難場所]]・[[指定避難所]]としての役割も担う{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=沼田市 |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}。]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|川原に残る遺構
川原の岩壺から温泉が湧き出ており、増水時は浴槽が水没していたという{{Sfn |群馬県 |1934 | p=355 }}。]] 利根町老神・利根町穴原・利根町大楊にまたがる{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=老神温泉旅館組合 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }}。[[片品川]]が作った深い[[渓谷]]に沿って[[温泉街]]が広がっている。1935年（昭和10年）古くから[[皮膚病]]に効く[[湯治場]]として知られ、[[大正]]時代には[[若山牧水]]が当地を訪れている。[[1935]]（昭和10年）、老神温泉・穴原温泉・大楊温泉の旅館が組合を結成し、「老神[[温泉郷]]」と命名。[[1959]]（昭和34年）には[[薗原ダム]]の建設に伴い、4軒が対岸の高台に移転し、1軒が取り壊しに遭った{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 宿泊施設は[[明治]]時代初期に1軒・浴場1であったものが、[[昭和]]初期になると十数軒、最盛期の[[1983]]（昭和58年）には22軒を数えた{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。老神温泉旅館組合公式ウェブサイトによると、2022年時点での旅館数は13軒である。 [[金精トンネル]]や[[関越自動車道]]の開通により、[[尾瀬]]や[[日光市|日光]]への観光の拠点としての機能も担うようになった。毎年10月が入込のピークである{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 == イベント == * 2月：老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 2月 - 3月：老神温泉びっくりひな飾り{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 4月 - 11月：老神温泉朝市{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 5月：老神温泉大蛇まつり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 6月：老神温泉ポピーまつり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 8月：とねふるさと風のまつり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 8月：老神温泉納涼花火大会{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * 9月：老神温泉そば祭り{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=沼田市 |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === 地名の由来 === 「老神」の地名の由来として、『[[角川日本地名大辞典]]』「老神」・「老神温泉」の項には次のような説が紹介されている{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}。 * [[赤城山]]の[[神]]と[[男体山|二荒山]]の神との争いで、赤城山の神が当地に敗走して来たこと（追神）から。あるいは、逆に赤城山の神が二荒山の神を追い返したこと（追神）から。 * 赤城山の神による開湯伝説（「湯の神」の転訛）から。 * 「お湯の上（かみ）」の転訛。 * 「老神」の表記は[[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]]年間に[[沼田城]]城主夫人が追神を訪れ、老い行く我が身とかけて「老が身」としたことから。 === 開湯伝説・神域 === 伝承によると赤城山の神と二荒山の神との間で領地を巡う争いがあり、やがて劣勢となって当地まで撤退を余儀なくされた赤城山の神によって温泉が開かれたとされる。赤城山の神が[[弓]]（あるいは[[矢]]）を立てた場所から温泉が湧出し、傷を癒やした赤城山の神は再び二荒山の神に挑み、打ち倒したという。[[古墳時代]]、「[[毛野|毛野国]]」が2分され、現在の群馬県（[[上野国]]）と[[栃木県]]（[[下野国]]）となる過程で起こった[[領土問題|国境争い]]から生まれた話とされる{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146 }}。 [[テレビアニメ]]『[[まんが日本昔ばなし]]』に見られるように、一般的には赤城山の神は大[[ムカデ]]、二荒山の神は[[ヘビ|大蛇]]とされる。しかし、老神温泉では赤城山の神が大蛇、二荒山の神が大ムカデと逆転して伝えられている{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}。これに関しては[[藤原秀郷]]のムカデ退治の話から、勝者を大蛇に、敗者を大ムカデに見立てるものだとすれば、老神温泉では赤城山の神が勝利したとされることから、勝利した赤城山の神が大蛇で、敗退した二荒山の神が大ムカデと言われるようになったとする研究がある{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147 }}。 因みに老神温泉に流れる片品川を挟んだ東側に二荒山神社、西側に赤城神社がある。 '''歌''' 老神おんど 老神は、いで湯町 == 交通アクセス == ;[[公共交通機関]] :[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[上越新幹線]]・[[上毛高原駅]]より[[路線バス]]（[[関越交通]]）で約70分間。 :JR[[上越線]]・[[沼田駅]]より路線バス（関越交通）で約50分間。 :{{要出典範囲|date=2021-04|一部のホテル・旅館では、上毛高原駅・沼田駅～老神温泉バス停間で、無料送迎[[バス (車両)|バス]]『じゃおう号』を共同運行している。対象は宿泊予約済みの客限定で、前日までに予約が必要。宿泊施設によっては同バスに参加していないところもある}}。 ;[[自家用自動車]] :[[関越自動車道]]・[[沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|沼田インターチェンジ]]より[[国道120号]]を約30分間。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1988 {{Japanese Hot Spring Resort |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Numata City|ja|沼田市}} {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|lt=Tone Town|lt_ja=利根町}} |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅}} → {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|lt=Bus|lt_ja=バス}} for 70 minutes
{{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅}} → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC}} → {{ill|Automobile|ja|自動車|lt=Car|lt_ja=車}} for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Numata City|ja|沼田市}}, and {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|lt=Tone Town|lt_ja=利根町}}. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|ja|セルシウス度|lt=Celsius|lt_ja=摂氏}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|ja|男体山|lt=Mount Futaarayama|lt_ja=二荒山}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|ja|沼田城}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keino|ja|毛野|lt=Keino Country|lt_ja=毛野国}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|centipede|ja|ムカデ}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|snake|ja|ヘビ|lt=Giant Snake|lt_ja=大蛇}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|藤原秀郷}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}’s {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} and {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Joetsu Line|ja|上越線}} and {{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{要出典範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ}}, take {{ill|National Route 120|ja|国道120号}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 {{Japanese Hot Spring Resort |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Numata City|ja|沼田市}} {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|lt=Tone Town|lt_ja=利根町}} |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅}} → {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|lt=Bus|lt_ja=バス}} for 70 minutes
{{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅}} → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC}} → {{ill|Automobile|ja|自動車|lt=Car|lt_ja=車}} for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Numata City|ja|沼田市}}, and {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|lt=Tone Town|lt_ja=利根町}}. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|ja|セルシウス度|lt=Celsius|lt_ja=摂氏}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|ja|男体山|lt=Mount Futaarayama|lt_ja=二荒山}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|ja|沼田城}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keino|ja|毛野|lt=Keino Country|lt_ja=毛野国}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|centipede|ja|ムカデ}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|snake|ja|ヘビ|lt=Giant Snake|lt_ja=大蛇}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|藤原秀郷}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}’s {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} and {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Joetsu Line|ja|上越線}} and {{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{要出典範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ}}, take {{ill|National Route 120|ja|国道120号}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC}} → {{ill|Automobile|ja|自動車|lt=Car|lt_ja=車}} for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Numata City|ja|沼田市}}, and {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|lt=Tone Town|lt_ja=利根町}}. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|ja|セルシウス度|lt=Celsius|lt_ja=摂氏}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|ja|男体山|lt=Mount Futaarayama|lt_ja=二荒山}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|ja|沼田城}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keino|ja|毛野|lt=Keino Country|lt_ja=毛野国}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|centipede|ja|ムカデ}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|snake|ja|ヘビ|lt=Giant Snake|lt_ja=大蛇}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|藤原秀郷}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}’s {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} and {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Joetsu Line|ja|上越線}} and {{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{要出典範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ}}, take {{ill|National Route 120|ja|国道120号}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|ja|男体山|lt=Mount Futaarayama|lt_ja=二荒山}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|ja|沼田城}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keino|ja|毛野|lt=Keino Country|lt_ja=毛野国}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|centipede|ja|ムカデ}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|snake|ja|ヘビ|lt=Giant Snake|lt_ja=大蛇}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|藤原秀郷}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}’s {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} and {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Joetsu Line|ja|上越線}} and {{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{要出典範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ}}, take {{ill|National Route 120|ja|国道120号}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 {{Japanese Hot Spring Resort |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Numata City|ja|沼田市}} {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|lt=Tone Town|lt_ja=利根町}} |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅}} → {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|lt=Bus|lt_ja=バス}} for 70 minutes
{{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅}} → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC}} → {{ill|Automobile|ja|自動車|lt=Car|lt_ja=車}} for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Numata City|ja|沼田市}}, and {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|lt=Tone Town|lt_ja=利根町}}. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|ja|セルシウス度|lt=Celsius|lt_ja=摂氏}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|ja|男体山|lt=Mount Futaarayama|lt_ja=二荒山}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|ja|沼田城}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keino|ja|毛野|lt=Keino Country|lt_ja=毛野国}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|centipede|ja|ムカデ}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|snake|ja|ヘビ|lt=Giant Snake|lt_ja=大蛇}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|藤原秀郷}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}. revision.text:
{{Japanese Hot Spring Resort |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Numata City|ja|沼田市}} {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|lt=Tone Town|lt_ja=利根町}} |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅}} → {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|lt=Bus|lt_ja=バス}} for 70 minutes
{{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅}} → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC}} → {{ill|Automobile|ja|自動車|lt=Car|lt_ja=車}} for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Numata City|ja|沼田市}}, and {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|lt=Tone Town|lt_ja=利根町}}. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|ja|セルシウス度|lt=Celsius|lt_ja=摂氏}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|ja|男体山|lt=Mount Futaarayama|lt_ja=二荒山}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|ja|沼田城}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keino|ja|毛野|lt=Keino Country|lt_ja=毛野国}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|centipede|ja|ムカデ}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|snake|ja|ヘビ|lt=Giant Snake|lt_ja=大蛇}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|藤原秀郷}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}’s {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線}} and {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Joetsu Line|ja|上越線}} and {{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{要出典範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ}}, take {{ill|National Route 120|ja|国道120号}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=角川書店 |year=1988 |isbn=4040011007 |ref={{harvid|『角川日本地名大辞典』}} }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=上毛大観 |author=群馬県 |publisher=煥乎堂 |year=1934 |url={{NDLDC|1224435}} |ref=harv }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=-ぐんま謎学の旅- 民話と伝説の舞台 |author=小暮淳 |publisher=ライフケア群栄 ちいきしんぶん |year=2018 |isbn=9784991038204 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=栗原久 |date=2014-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.57530/00000149 |title=人々を楽しませる赤城山の魅力 2.赤城山をめぐる伝説とそのルーツの考察 |journal=東京福祉大学・大学院紀要 |ISSN=1883-7565 |publisher=東京福祉大学・大学院 |volume=4 |issue=2 |pages=155-167 |doi=10.57530/00000149 |naid=120005486690 |CRID=1390577309351894016 |ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Gyoza no Manshu|ja|ぎょうざの満洲}} * {{ill|Tone District|ja|利根郡}} and {{ill|Higashi Village|ja|東村 (群馬県利根郡)|lt=Higashi Village|lt_ja=東村}} – see also {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村}} * {{ill|Hokusei Tago District|ja|北勢多郡}} – {{ill|Akaginemura|ja|赤城根村}} * {{ill|Fukiware Waterfall|ja|吹割の滝}} * {{ill|Shiizaka Pass|ja|椎坂峠}} * {{ill|Romantic Road (Japan)|ja|日本ロマンチック街道}} == External Links == * [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] * [http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/kanko/tone/1001830.html Oigami Onsen] – Numata City * [https://twitter.com/oigamijyaou Jaou‑kun (Oigami Onsen Character)] – {{ill|Twitter|ja|Twitter}} {{Hot Spring}}
