The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|ja|セルシウス度|lt=Celsius|lt_ja=摂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celsius|qq=}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Akagi|qq=}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|ja|男体山|lt=Mount Futaarayama|lt_ja=二荒山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nantai|qq=}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|ja|沼田城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Castle|qq=}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keino|ja|毛野|lt=Keino Country|lt_ja=毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keino|qq=}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|qq=}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|centipede|ja|ムカデ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/centipede|qq=}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|snake|ja|ヘビ|lt=Giant Snake|lt_ja=大蛇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snake|qq=}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|qq=}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|qq=}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}’s {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joetsu Shinkansen|qq=}} and {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomo Kogen Station|qq=}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|qq=}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Joetsu Line|ja|上越線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joetsu Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Station|qq=}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{要出典範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Expressway|qq=}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}}, take {{ill|National Route 120|ja|国道120号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 120|qq=}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Akagi|qq=}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|ja|男体山|lt=Mount Futaarayama|lt_ja=二荒山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nantai|qq=}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|ja|沼田城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Castle|qq=}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keino|ja|毛野|lt=Keino Country|lt_ja=毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keino|qq=}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|qq=}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|centipede|ja|ムカデ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/centipede|qq=}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|snake|ja|ヘビ|lt=Giant Snake|lt_ja=大蛇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snake|qq=}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|qq=}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|qq=}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}’s {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joetsu Shinkansen|qq=}} and {{ill|Jomo Kogen Station|ja|上毛高原駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomo Kogen Station|qq=}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|qq=}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Joetsu Line|ja|上越線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joetsu Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Numata Station|ja|沼田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Station|qq=}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{Citation needed範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Expressway|ja|関越自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Expressway|qq=}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}}, take {{ill|National Route 120|ja|国道120号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 120|qq=}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=角川書店 |year=1988 |isbn=4040011007 |ref={{harvid|『角川日本地名大辞典』}} }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=上毛大観 |author=群馬県 |publisher=煥乎堂 |year=1934 |url={{NDLDC|1224435}} |ref=harv }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=-ぐんま謎学の旅- 民話と伝説の舞台 |author=小暮淳 |publisher=ライフケア群栄 ちいきしんぶん |year=2018 |isbn=9784991038204 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=栗原久 |date=2014-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.57530/00000149 |title=人々を楽しませる赤城山の魅力 2.赤城山をめぐる伝説とそのルーツの考察 |journal=東京福祉大学・大学院紀要 |ISSN=1883-7565 |publisher=東京福祉大学・大学院 |volume=4 |issue=2 |pages=155-167 |doi=10.57530/00000149 |naid=120005486690 |CRID=1390577309351894016 |ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Gyoza no Manshu|ja|ぎょうざの満洲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyoza no Manshu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tone District|ja|利根郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone District|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashi Village|ja|東村 (群馬県利根郡)|lt=Higashi Village|lt_ja=東村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Village|qq=}} – see also {{ill|Tone Village|ja|利根村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone Village|qq=}} * {{ill|Hokusei Tago District|ja|北勢多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokusei Tago District|qq=}} – {{ill|Akaginemura|ja|赤城根村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaginemura|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukiware Waterfall|ja|吹割の滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiware Waterfall|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiizaka Pass|ja|椎坂峠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiizaka Pass|qq=}} * {{ill|Romantic Road (Japan)|ja|日本ロマンチック街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Romantic Road (Japan)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] * [http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/kanko/tone/1001830.html Oigami Onsen] – Numata City * [https://twitter.com/oigamijyaou Jaou‑kun (Oigami Onsen Character)] – {{ill|Twitter|ja|Twitter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twitter|qq=}} {{Onsen}} {{translated {{infobox Onsen |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] [[:en:Numata, Gunma|Numata City]] [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Town]] |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = [[:en:Jōmō-Kōgen Station|Jomo Kogen Station]] → [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]] for 70 minutes
[[:en:Numata Station|Numata Station]] → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}} → [[:en:Car|Car]] for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Numata, Gunma|Numata City]], and [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Town]]. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 [[:en:Celsius|Celsius]] * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] and the god of [[:en:Mount Nantai|Mount Futaarayama]], in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of [[:en:Numata Castle|Numata Castle]] visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of [[:en:Keno Province|Keino Country]], in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|qq=}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant [[:en:Centipede|centipede]], and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant [[:en:Snake|Giant Snake]]. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|qq=}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|qq=}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the [[:en:Kan-etsu Expressway|Kan-Etsu Expressway]] and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}}, take [[:en:Japan National Route 120|National Route 120]] for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=角川書店 |year=1988 |isbn=4040011007 |ref={{harvid|『角川日本地名大辞典』}} }} * |isbn=9784991038204 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=栗原久 |date=2014-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.57530/00000149 |title=人々を楽しませる赤城山の魅力 2.赤城山をめぐる伝説とそのルーツの考察 |journal=東京福祉大学・大学院紀要 |ISSN=1883-7565 revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] [[:en:Numata, Gunma|Numata City]] [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Town]] |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = [[:en:Jōmō-Kōgen Station|Jomo Kogen Station]] → [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]] for 70 minutes
[[:en:Numata Station|Numata Station]] → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}} → [[:en:Car|Car]] for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Numata, Gunma|Numata City]], and [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Town]]. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 [[:en:Celsius|Celsius]] * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] and the god of [[:en:Mount Nantai|Mount Futaarayama]], in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of [[:en:Numata Castle|Numata Castle]] visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of [[:en:Keno Province|Keino Country]], in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|qq=}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant [[:en:Centipede|centipede]], and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant [[:en:Snake|Giant Snake]]. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|qq=}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|qq=}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]’s [[:en:Jōetsu Shinkansen|Joetsu Shinkansen]] and [[:en:Jōmō-Kōgen Station|Jomo Kogen Station]], take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|qq=}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR [[:en:Jōetsu Line|Joetsu Line]] and [[:en:Numata Station|Numata Station]], take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{Citation needed範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the [[:en:Kan-etsu Expressway|Kan-Etsu Expressway]] and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}}, take [[:en:Japan National Route 120|National Route 120]] for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=角川書店 |year=1988 |isbn=4040011007 |ref={{harvid|『角川日本地名大辞典』}} }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=上毛大観 |author=群馬県 |publisher=煥乎堂 |year=1934 |url={{NDLDC|1224435}} |ref=harv }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=-ぐんま謎学の旅- 民話と伝説の舞台 |author=小暮淳 |publisher=ライフケア群栄 ちいきしんぶん |year=2018 |isbn=9784991038204 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=栗原久 |date=2014-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.57530/00000149 |title=人々を楽しませる赤城山の魅力 2.赤城山をめぐる伝説とそのルーツの考察 |journal=東京福祉大学・大学院紀要 |ISSN=1883-7565 |publisher=東京福祉大学・大学院 |volume=4 |issue=2 |pages=155-167 |doi=10.57530/00000149 |naid=120005486690 |CRID=1390577309351894016 |ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Gyoza no Manshu|ja|ぎょうざの満洲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyoza no Manshu|qq=}} * [[:en:Tone District, Gunma|Tone District]] and [[:en:Azuma, Gunma (Tone)|Higashi Village]] – see also [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Village]] * [[:en:Kitaseta District, Gunma|Hokusei Tago District]] – [[:en:Akagine, Gunma|Akaginemura]] * [[:en:Fukiware Falls|Fukiware Waterfall]] * {{ill|Shiizaka Pass|ja|椎坂峠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiizaka Pass|qq=}} * [[:en:Japan Romantic Road|Romantic Road (Japan)]] == External Links == * [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] * [http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/kanko/tone/1001830.html Oigami Onsen] – Numata City * [https://twitter.com/oigamijyaou Jaou‑kun (Oigami Onsen Character)] – {{ill|Twitter|ja|Twitter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twitter|qq=}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|老神温泉|version=98250726|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| Origin Myth]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[en:Oigami Onsen]] [[Category:沼田市の地理]]

It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] and the god of [[:en:Mount Nantai|Mount Futaarayama]], in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of [[:en:Numata Castle|Numata Castle]] visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of [[:en:Keno Province|Keino Country]], in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|qq=}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant [[:en:Centipede|centipede]], and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant [[:en:Snake|Giant Snake]]. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|qq=}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|qq=}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]’s [[:en:Jōetsu Shinkansen|Joetsu Shinkansen]] and [[:en:Jōmō-Kōgen Station|Jomo Kogen Station]], take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|qq=}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR [[:en:Jōetsu Line|Joetsu Line]] and [[:en:Numata Station|Numata Station]], take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{Citation needed範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the [[:en:Kan-etsu Expressway|Kan-Etsu Expressway]] and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}}, take [[:en:Japan National Route 120|National Route 120]] for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=角川書店 |year=1988 |isbn=4040011007 |ref={{harvid|『角川日本地名大辞典』}} }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=上毛大観 |author=群馬県 |publisher=煥乎堂 |year=1934 |url={{NDLDC|1224435}} |ref=harv }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=-ぐんま謎学の旅- 民話と伝説の舞台 |author=小暮淳 |publisher=ライフケア群栄 ちいきしんぶん |year=2018 |isbn=9784991038204 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=栗原久 |date=2014-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.57530/00000149 |title=人々を楽しませる赤城山の魅力 2.赤城山をめぐる伝説とそのルーツの考察 |journal=東京福祉大学・大学院紀要 |ISSN=1883-7565 |publisher=東京福祉大学・大学院 |volume=4 |issue=2 |pages=155-167 |doi=10.57530/00000149 |naid=120005486690 |CRID=1390577309351894016 |ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Gyoza no Manshu|ja|ぎょうざの満洲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyoza no Manshu|qq=}} * [[:en:Tone District, Gunma|Tone District]] and [[:en:Azuma, Gunma (Tone)|Higashi Village]] – see also [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Village]] * [[:en:Kitaseta District, Gunma|Hokusei Tago District]] – [[:en:Akagine, Gunma|Akaginemura]] * [[:en:Fukiware Falls|Fukiware Waterfall]] * {{ill|Shiizaka Pass|ja|椎坂峠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiizaka Pass|qq=}} * [[:en:Japan Romantic Road|Romantic Road (Japan)]] == External Links == * [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] * [http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/kanko/tone/1001830.html Oigami Onsen] – Numata City * [https://twitter.com/oigamijyaou Jaou‑kun (Oigami Onsen Character)] – revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] [[:en:Numata, Gunma|Numata City]] [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Town]] |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = [[:en:Jōmō-Kōgen Station|Jomo Kogen Station]] → [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]] for 70 minutes
[[:en:Numata Station|Numata Station]] → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}} → [[:en:Car|Car]] for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Numata, Gunma|Numata City]], and [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Town]]. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 [[:en:Celsius|Celsius]] * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] and the god of [[:en:Mount Nantai|Mount Futaarayama]], in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of [[:en:Numata Castle|Numata Castle]] visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of [[:en:Keno Province|Keino Country]], in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|qq=}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant [[:en:Centipede|centipede]], and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant [[:en:Snake|Giant Snake]]. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|qq=}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|qq=}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]’s [[:en:Jōetsu Shinkansen|Joetsu Shinkansen]] and [[:en:Jōmō-Kōgen Station|Jomo Kogen Station]], take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|qq=}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR [[:en:Jōetsu Line|Joetsu Line]] and [[:en:Numata Station|Numata Station]], take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{Citation needed範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the [[:en:Kan-etsu Expressway|Kan-Etsu Expressway]] and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}}, take [[:en:Japan National Route 120|National Route 120]] for approximately 30 minutes. == |isbn=9784991038204 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=栗原久 |date=2014-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.57530/00000149 |title=人々を楽しませる赤城山の魅力 2.赤城山をめぐる伝説とそのルーツの考察 |journal=東京福祉大学・大学院紀要 |ISSN=1883-7565 |publisher=東京福祉大学・大学院 |volume=4 |issue=2 |pages=155-167 |doi=10.57530/00000149 |naid=120005486690 |CRID=1390577309351894016 |ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Gyoza no {{infobox Onsen |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] [[:en:Numata, Gunma|Numata City]] [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Town]] |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = [[:en:Jōmō-Kōgen Station|Jomo Kogen Station]] → [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]] for 70 minutes
[[:en:Numata Station|Numata Station]] → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}} → [[:en:Car|Car]] for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Numata, Gunma|Numata City]], and [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Town]]. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 [[:en:Celsius|Celsius]] * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582‑seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|qq=}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] and the god of [[:en:Mount Nantai|Mount Futaarayama]], in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of [[:en:Numata Castle|Numata Castle]] visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a [[bow]] (or [[arrow]]), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of [[:en:Keno Province|Keino Country]], in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|qq=}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant [[:en:Centipede|centipede]], and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant [[:en:Snake|Giant Snake]]. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|qq=}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|qq=}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]’s [[:en:Jōetsu Shinkansen|Joetsu Shinkansen]] and [[:en:Jōmō-Kōgen Station|Jomo Kogen Station]], take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|qq=}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR [[:en:Jōetsu Line|Joetsu Line]] and [[:en:Numata Station|Numata Station]], take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{Citation needed範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the [[:en:Kan-etsu Expressway|Kan-Etsu Expressway]] and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|qq=}}, take [[:en:Japan National Route 120|National Route 120]] for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=角川書店 |year=1988 |isbn=4040011007 |ref={{harvid|『角川日本地名大辞典』}} }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=上毛大観 |author=群馬県 |publisher=煥乎堂 |year=1934 |url={{NDLDC|1224435}} |ref=harv }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=-ぐんま謎学の旅- 民話と伝説の舞台 |author=小暮淳 |publisher=ライフケア群栄 ちいきしんぶん |year=2018 |isbn=9784991038204 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=栗原久 |date=2014-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.57530/00000149 |title=人々を楽しませる赤城山の魅力 2.赤城山をめぐる伝説とそのルーツの考察 |journal=東京福祉大学・大学院紀要 |ISSN=1883-7565 |publisher=東京福祉大学・大学院 |volume=4 |issue=2 |pages=155-167 |doi=10.57530/00000149 |naid=120005486690 |CRID=1390577309351894016 |ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Gyoza no Manshu|ja|ぎょうざの満洲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyoza no Manshu|qq=}} * [[:en:Tone District, Gunma|Tone District]] and [[:en:Azuma, Gunma (Tone)|Higashi Village]] – see also [[:en:Tone, Gunma|Tone Village]] * [[:en:Kitaseta District, Gunma|Hokusei Tago District]] – [[:en:Akagine, Gunma|Akaginemura]] * [[:en:Fukiware Falls|Fukiware Waterfall]] * {{ill|Shiizaka Pass|ja|椎坂峠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiizaka Pass|qq=}} * [[:en:Japan Romantic Road|Romantic Road (Japan)]] == External Links == * [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] * [http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/kanko/tone/1001830.html Oigami Onsen] – Numata City * [https://twitter.com/oigamijyaou Jaou‑kun (Oigami Onsen Character)] –
