[[co:commons:Category:Oigami Onsen]][[ja:老神温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Oigami Onsen]][[ja:老神温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oigami Onsen]][[ja:老神温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|WD=Q666317|lt=Numata City|1=Numata, Gunma}} {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Town|1=Tone, Gunma}} |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = {{ill|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|en|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|WD=Q1138087|lt=Jomo Kogen Station|1=Jōmō-Kōgen Station}} → {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Bus|1=Transit bus}} for 70 minutes
{{ill|Numata Station|en|Numata Station|WD=Q1706459|lt=Numata Station|1=Numata Station}} → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|1=Numata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11142871}} → {{ill|Car|en|Car|WD=Q1420|lt=Car|1=Car}} for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|WD=Q666317|lt=Numata City|1=Numata, Gunma}}, and {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Town|1=Tone, Gunma}}. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|en|Celsius|WD=Q25267|lt=Celsius|1=Celsius}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[:en:liters|liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582-seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keino Country|1=Keno Province}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|1=Manga Nippon Mukashibanashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9164362}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|Centipede|en|Centipede|WD=Q43447|lt=centipede|1=Centipede}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|Snake|en|Snake|WD=Q2102|lt=Giant Snake|1=Snake}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. [[co:commons:Category:Oigami Onsen]][[ja:老神温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Oigami Onsen]][[ja:老神温泉]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Oigami Onsen]][[ja:老神温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|WD=Q666317|lt=Numata City|1=Numata, Gunma}} {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Town|1=Tone, Gunma}} |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = {{ill|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|en|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|WD=Q1138087|lt=Jomo Kogen Station|1=Jōmō-Kōgen Station}} → {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Bus|1=Transit bus}} for 70 minutes
{{ill|Numata Station|en|Numata Station|WD=Q1706459|lt=Numata Station|1=Numata Station}} → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|1=Numata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11142871}} → {{ill|Car|en|Car|WD=Q1420|lt=Car|1=Car}} for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|WD=Q666317|lt=Numata City|1=Numata, Gunma}}, and {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Town|1=Tone, Gunma}}. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|en|Celsius|WD=Q25267|lt=Celsius|1=Celsius}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[:en:liters|liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582-seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|en|Mount Nantai|WD=Q1964765|lt=Mount Futaarayama|1=Mount Nantai}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|en|Numata Castle|WD=Q11554573|lt=Numata Castle|1=Numata Castle}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a {{ill|bow|en|bow|WD=Q401865|lt=bow|1=Bow}} (or {{ill|Arrow|en|Arrow|WD=Q45922|lt=Arrow|1=Arrow}}), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keino Country|1=Keno Province}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|1=Manga Nippon Mukashibanashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9164362}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|Centipede|en|Centipede|WD=Q43447|lt=centipede|1=Centipede}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|Snake|en|Snake|WD=Q2102|lt=Giant Snake|1=Snake}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. [[co:commons:Category:Oigami Onsen]][[ja:老神温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Oigami Onsen]][[ja:老神温泉]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Oigami Onsen]][[ja:老神温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|WD=Q666317|lt=Numata City|1=Numata, Gunma}} {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Town|1=Tone, Gunma}} |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = {{ill|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|en|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|WD=Q1138087|lt=Jomo Kogen Station|1=Jōmō-Kōgen Station}} → {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Bus|1=Transit bus}} for 70 minutes
{{ill|Numata Station|en|Numata Station|WD=Q1706459|lt=Numata Station|1=Numata Station}} → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|1=Numata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11142871}} → {{ill|Car|en|Car|WD=Q1420|lt=Car|1=Car}} for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|WD=Q666317|lt=Numata City|1=Numata, Gunma}}, and {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Town|1=Tone, Gunma}}. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|en|Celsius|WD=Q25267|lt=Celsius|1=Celsius}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[:en:liters|liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582-seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|en|Mount Nantai|WD=Q1964765|lt=Mount Futaarayama|1=Mount Nantai}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|en|Numata Castle|WD=Q11554573|lt=Numata Castle|1=Numata Castle}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a {{ill|bow|en|bow|WD=Q401865|lt=bow|1=Bow}} (or {{ill|Arrow|en|Arrow|WD=Q45922|lt=Arrow|1=Arrow}}), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keino Country|1=Keno Province}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|1=Manga Nippon Mukashibanashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9164362}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|Centipede|en|Centipede|WD=Q43447|lt=centipede|1=Centipede}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|Snake|en|Snake|WD=Q2102|lt=Giant Snake|1=Snake}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}’s {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|WD=Q912219|lt=Joetsu Shinkansen|1=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|en|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|WD=Q1138087|lt=Jomo Kogen Station|1=Jōmō-Kōgen Station}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|1=Kan'etsu Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656435}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Joetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}} and {{ill|Numata Station|en|Numata Station|WD=Q1706459|lt=Numata Station|1=Numata Station}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{Citation needed範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|1=Numata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11142871}}, take {{ill|Japan National Route 120|en|Japan National Route 120|WD=Q5362009|lt=National Route 120|1=Japan National Route 120}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|en|Celsius|WD=Q25267|lt=Celsius|1=Celsius}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[:en:liters|liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582-seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|en|Mount Nantai|WD=Q1964765|lt=Mount Futaarayama|1=Mount Nantai}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|en|Numata Castle|WD=Q11554573|lt=Numata Castle|1=Numata Castle}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a {{ill|bow|en|bow|WD=Q401865|lt=bow|1=Bow}} (or {{ill|Arrow|en|Arrow|WD=Q45922|lt=Arrow|1=Arrow}}), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keino Country|1=Keno Province}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|1=Manga Nippon Mukashibanashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9164362}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|Centipede|en|Centipede|WD=Q43447|lt=centipede|1=Centipede}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|Snake|en|Snake|WD=Q2102|lt=Giant Snake|1=Snake}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}’s {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|WD=Q912219|lt=Joetsu Shinkansen|1=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|en|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|WD=Q1138087|lt=Jomo Kogen Station|1=Jōmō-Kōgen Station}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|1=Kan'etsu Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656435}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Joetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}} and {{ill|Numata Station|en|Numata Station|WD=Q1706459|lt=Numata Station|1=Numata Station}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{Citation needed範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|1=Numata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11142871}}, take {{ill|Japan National Route 120|en|Japan National Route 120|WD=Q5362009|lt=National Route 120|1=Japan National Route 120}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=角川書店 |year=1988 |isbn=4040011007 |ref={{harvid|『角川日本地名大辞典』}} }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=上毛大観 |author=群馬県 |publisher=煥乎堂 |year=1934 |url={{NDLDC|1224435}} |ref=harv }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=-ぐんま謎学の旅- 民話と伝説の舞台 |author=小暮淳 |publisher=ライフケア群栄 ちいきしんぶん |year=2018 |isbn=9784991038204 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=栗原久 |date=2014-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.57530/00000149 |title=人々を楽しませる赤城山の魅力 2.赤城山をめぐる伝説とそのルーツの考察 |journal=東京福祉大学・大学院紀要 |ISSN=1883-7565 |publisher=東京福祉大学・大学院 |volume=4 |issue=2 |pages=155-167 |doi=10.57530/00000149 |naid=120005486690 |CRID=1390577309351894016 |ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Gyoza no Manshu|ja|ぎょうざの満洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyoza no Manshu|1=Gyoza no Manshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265292}} * {{ill|Tone District, Gunma|en|Tone District, Gunma|WD=Q1077001|lt=Tone District|1=Tone District, Gunma}} and {{ill|Azuma, Gunma (Tone)|en|Azuma, Gunma (Tone)|WD=Q4833051|lt=Higashi Village|1=Azuma, Gunma (Tone)}} – see also {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Village|1=Tone, Gunma}} * {{ill|Kitaseta District, Gunma|en|Kitaseta District, Gunma|WD=Q11401182|lt=Hokusei Tago District|1=Kitaseta District, Gunma}} – {{ill|Akagine, Gunma|en|Akagine, Gunma|WD=Q4700144|lt=Akaginemura|1=Akagine, Gunma}} * {{ill|Fukiware Falls|en|Fukiware Falls|WD=Q11416484|lt=Fukiware Waterfall|1=Fukiware Falls}} * {{ill|Shiizaka Pass|ja|椎坂峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiizaka Pass|1=Shiisaka Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540833}} * {{ill|Japan Romantic Road|en|Japan Romantic Road|WD=Q11507056|lt=Romantic Road (Japan)|1=Japan Romantic Road}} == External Links == * [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] * [http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/kanko/tone/1001830.html Oigami Onsen] – Numata City * [https://twitter.com/oigamijyaou Jaou-kun (Oigami Onsen Character)] – occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} presents the following theories{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}: * It may derive from a conflict between the god of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} and the god of {{ill|Mount Nantai|en|Mount Nantai|WD=Q1964765|lt=Mount Futaarayama|1=Mount Nantai}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|en|Numata Castle|WD=Q11554573|lt=Numata Castle|1=Numata Castle}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. It is said that hot spring water began to flow from the spot where the god of Mount Akagi set up a {{ill|bow|en|bow|WD=Q401865|lt=bow|1=Bow}} (or {{ill|Arrow|en|Arrow|WD=Q45922|lt=Arrow|1=Arrow}}), and after healing his wounds, he challenged and defeated the god of Mount Nantai. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keino Country|1=Keno Province}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|1=Manga Nippon Mukashibanashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9164362}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|Centipede|en|Centipede|WD=Q43447|lt=centipede|1=Centipede}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|Snake|en|Snake|WD=Q2102|lt=Giant Snake|1=Snake}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}’s {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|WD=Q912219|lt=Joetsu Shinkansen|1=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|en|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|WD=Q1138087|lt=Jomo Kogen Station|1=Jōmō-Kōgen Station}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|1=Kan'etsu Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656435}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Joetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}} and {{ill|Numata Station|en|Numata Station|WD=Q1706459|lt=Numata Station|1=Numata Station}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{Citation needed範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|1=Numata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11142871}}, take {{ill|Japan National Route 120|en|Japan National Route 120|WD=Q5362009|lt=National Route 120|1=Japan National Route 120}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |和書 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 10 群馬県 |author=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会、[[竹内理三]] |publisher=角川書店 |year=1988 |isbn=4040011007 |ref={{harvid|『角川日本地名大辞典』}} }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=上毛大観 |author=群馬県 |publisher=煥乎堂 |year=1934 |url={{NDLDC|1224435}} |ref=harv }} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=-ぐんま謎学の旅- 民話と伝説の舞台 |author=小暮淳 |publisher=ライフケア群栄 ちいきしんぶん |year=2018 |isbn=9784991038204 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=栗原久 |date=2014-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.57530/00000149 |title=人々を楽しませる赤城山の魅力 2.赤城山をめぐる伝説とそのルーツの考察 |journal=東京福祉大学・大学院紀要 |ISSN=1883-7565 |publisher=東京福祉大学・大学院 |volume=4 |issue=2 |pages=155-167 |doi=10.57530/00000149 |naid=120005486690 |CRID=1390577309351894016 |ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Gyoza no Manshu|ja|ぎょうざの満洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyoza no Manshu|1=Gyoza no Manshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265292}} * {{ill|Tone District, Gunma|en|Tone District, Gunma|WD=Q1077001|lt=Tone District|1=Tone District, Gunma}} and {{ill|Azuma, Gunma (Tone)|en|Azuma, Gunma (Tone)|WD=Q4833051|lt=Higashi Village|1=Azuma, Gunma (Tone)}} – see also {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Village|1=Tone, Gunma}} * {{ill|Kitaseta District, Gunma|en|Kitaseta District, Gunma|WD=Q11401182|lt=Hokusei Tago District|1=Kitaseta District, Gunma}} – {{ill|Akagine, Gunma|en|Akagine, Gunma|WD=Q4700144|lt=Akaginemura|1=Akagine, Gunma}} * {{ill|Fukiware Falls|en|Fukiware Falls|WD=Q11416484|lt=Fukiware Waterfall|1=Fukiware Falls}} * {{ill|Shiizaka Pass|ja|椎坂峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiizaka Pass|1=Shiisaka Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540833}} * {{ill|Japan Romantic Road|en|Japan Romantic Road|WD=Q11507056|lt=Romantic Road (Japan)|1=Japan Romantic Road}} == External Links == * [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] * [http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/kanko/tone/1001830.html Oigami Onsen] – Numata City * [https://twitter.com/oigamijyaou Jaou-kun (Oigami Onsen Character)] – revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Oigami Onsen |image = Oigami Onsen view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg |Comment = Summer Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in July 2016) |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|WD=Q666317|lt=Numata City|1=Numata, Gunma}} {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Town|1=Tone, Gunma}} |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 40 |Latitude Seconds = 16 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 12 |Longitude Seconds = 35 |Access = {{ill|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|en|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|WD=Q1138087|lt=Jomo Kogen Station|1=Jōmō-Kōgen Station}} → {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Bus|1=Transit bus}} for 70 minutes
{{ill|Numata Station|en|Numata Station|WD=Q1706459|lt=Numata Station|1=Numata Station}} → Bus for 50 minutes
{{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata IC|lt_ja=沼田IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|1=Numata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11142871}} → {{ill|Car|en|Car|WD=Q1420|lt=Car|1=Car}} for 30 minutes{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association|accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Water Type = (Representative) {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = (Representative) 61.5 |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = 13 |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = 165,355 (2019 estimate){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/shisei/profile/toukei/1010723.html |title=Numata City Statistical Book (Reiwa 2 Edition) |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-03-17 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }} |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://www.oigami.net/ Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association] |Special Notes = }} [[File:Oigami-Onsen Autumn.JPG|thumb|Autumn Oigami Onsen Resort (photographed in November 2011)]] '''Oigami Onsen''' (老神温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|WD=Q666317|lt=Numata City|1=Numata, Gunma}}, and {{ill|Tone, Gunma|en|Tone, Gunma|WD=Q7820818|lt=Tone Town|1=Tone, Gunma}}. == Water Quality == According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, Oigami Onsen has eight hot spring sources. The representative characteristics of these sources are as follows: * Water Type: {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} * Temperature: 61.5 {{ill|Celsius|en|Celsius|WD=Q25267|lt=Celsius|1=Celsius}} * Benefits: Relief for dry skin conditions (atopic dermatitis), chronic rheumatoid arthritis, muscle pain, neuralgia, nerve inflammation, wounds, and urticaria, among others The eight sources are: * Oigami Source No. 1 * Oigami Source No. 3 * Oigami Source No. 5 * Oigami Source No. 7 * Oigami Source No. 8 * Oigami Source No. 10 * Kannon Yakuyu * Wakanoyu Source No. 3 Previously, there were 15 sources with a discharge of 1150 [[:en:liters|liters]] per minute{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:Tone Kanko Kaikan.jpg|thumb|Numata City Tone Tourism Hall (607, Oigami Onsen, Tone Town) – A facility established for the promotion of tourism and local industries{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/numata/reiki_honbun/e207RG00000678.html |title=沼田市利根観光会館の設置及び管理に関する条例 |publisher=Numata City |date=2019-09-10 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. It houses a 582-seat main hall and conference rooms{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oze.or.jp/~yukemuri/bunka/a/a020802.htm |title=沼田市利根観光会館 |publisher=利根沼田広域市町村圏振興整備組合 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}. In emergencies, it also serves as a designated evacuation site and shelter{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/life/anzen/sonaeru/1006062/1006070/1006211.html |title=利根観光会館（指定緊急避難場所・指定避難所） |publisher=Numata City |date=2016-11-29 |accessdate=2021-04-10 }}]] [[File:Katashina River view from Nairaku Bridge.jpg|thumb|Remnants along the riverbank – Hot spring water emerges from rock basins in the riverbed, and during flood conditions the bathtubs were submerged{{Sfn|群馬県|1934|p=355}}.]] The onsen town spans the areas of Oigami, Anahara, and Taiyō in Tone Town{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.oigami.net/yado.html |title=老神温泉の宿 |publisher=Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} and extends along the deep valley carved by the Katashina River. Since 1935 (Showa 10), the area has been renowned as a hot spring resort effective for treating skin diseases, with Makisui Wakayama having visited during the Taisho era. In 1935 (Showa 10), the ryokan of Oigami Onsen, Anahara Onsen, and Taiyō Onsen formed an association and named the area "Oigami Onsen Resort." In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the construction of the Sonohara Dam, four establishments were relocated to a hillside on the opposite bank while one was demolished{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. Accommodations began as a single ryokan with one bath in the early Meiji era, expanded to over a dozen in the early Showa period, and peaked at 22 establishments in 1983 (Showa 58){{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. According to the official website of the Oigami Onsen Ryokan Association, as of 2022 there are 13 ryokan. With the opening of the Kinsei Tunnel and the Kan‐Etsu Expressway, the area has also come to serve as a gateway for tourism to regions such as Oze and Nikko, with October being the peak month for occupancy{{Sfn|『角川日本地名大辞典』|p=191}}. == Events == * February: Oigami Onsen Setsubun Festival and Winter Fireworks{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1006562.html |title=老神温泉節分祭・冬の花火 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * February – March: Oigami Onsen Surprise Hina Doll Display{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1010521.html |title=老神温泉びっくりひな飾り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * April – November: Oigami Onsen Morning Market{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003719.html |title=老神温泉朝市 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * May: Oigami Onsen Giant Snake Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1009892.html |title=老神温泉大蛇まつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * June: Oigami Onsen Poppy Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1003932.html |title=老神温泉ポピーまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Tone Hometown Wind Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004185.html |title=とねふるさと風のまつり |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * August: Oigami Onsen Summer Cooling Fireworks Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1004221.html |title=老神温泉納涼花火大会 |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} * September: Oigami Onsen Soba Festival{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.numata.gunma.jp/event/tone/1005907.html |title=老神温泉そば祭り |publisher=Numata City |date=2021-04-01 |accessdate=2021-04-08 }} == History == === Origin of the Place Name === Regarding the origin of the name "Oigami," the entry for "Oigami" and "Oigami Onsen" in the {{ill|Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} presents the Nantai|en|Mount Nantai|WD=Q1964765|lt=Mount Futaarayama|1=Mount Nantai}}, in which the god of Mount Akagi was forced to retreat into this area (the "chasing god") – or conversely, from his repulsion of the other. * It may stem from the hot spring founding legend attributed to the god of Mount Akagi (a corruption of "Yunokami," the god of hot springs). * It may be a corruption of "Oyu no ue" (meaning "above the hot water"). * The name "Oigami" is said to have been coined during the Eiroku era when the wife of the lord of {{ill|Numata Castle|en|Numata Castle|WD=Q11554573|lt=Numata Castle|1=Numata Castle}} visited the chasing god and, reflecting on her aging self, remarked "old is my body." === Hot Spring Founding Legend and Sacred Area === According to legend, a territorial dispute between the god of Mount Akagi and the god of Mount Nantai led to the former being forced to retreat to this area, where he then opened the hot spring. This tale is believed to have originated from a border dispute during the division of the ancient country of {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keino Country|1=Keno Province}}, in the Kofun period, which eventually resulted in the formation of present-day Gunma Prefecture (formerly Ueno Province) and Tochigi Prefecture (formerly Shimono Province){{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=146}}. As depicted in the television anime {{ill|Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|ja|まんが日本昔ばなし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manga Nihon Mukashi Banashi|1=Manga Nippon Mukashibanashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9164362}}, it is generally held that the god of Mount Akagi is represented as a giant {{ill|Centipede|en|Centipede|WD=Q43447|lt=centipede|1=Centipede}}, and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant {{ill|Snake|en|Snake|WD=Q2102|lt=Giant Snake|1=Snake}}. However, at Oigami Onsen the tradition is reversed, with the god of Mount Akagi portrayed as a giant snake and the god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|小暮淳|2018|p=69}}. In this context, some studies suggest that—drawing from the story of {{ill|Fujiwara Hidego|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidego|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}’s centipede exorcism—the victor is likened to a giant snake while the vanquished is compared to a giant centipede; since the god of Mount Akagi is said to have triumphed at Oigami Onsen, he is depicted as a giant snake and the defeated god of Mount Nantai as a giant centipede{{Sfn|栗原久|2014|p=147}}. Incidentally, along the Katashina River that flows through Oigami Onsen, the east side is home to the {{ill|Futaarakasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarakasan Shrine|1=Futaarayama shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95932360}}, while the west side hosts the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}. '''Song''' Oigami Ondo Oigami is the town of Ideyu == Access == ;'''Public Transportation''' : From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}’s {{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|WD=Q912219|lt=Joetsu Shinkansen|1=Jōetsu Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|en|Jōmō-Kōgen Station|WD=Q1138087|lt=Jomo Kogen Station|1=Jōmō-Kōgen Station}}, take the local bus operated by {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|1=Kan'etsu Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656435}} for about 70 minutes. : From the JR {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|WD=Q1135061|lt=Joetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}} and {{ill|Numata Station|en|Numata Station|WD=Q1706459|lt=Numata Station|1=Numata Station}}, take a local bus (Kan-Etsu Kōtsū) for about 50 minutes. :{{Citation needed範囲|date=2021-04|Some hotels and ryokan operate a free shuttle bus called “Jaou-go” between Jomo Kogen Station/Numata Station and the Oigami Onsen bus stop. This service is available only to guests with confirmed reservations and must be booked by the day before. Not all accommodations participate in this service.}} ;'''Private Automobile''' : From the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} and {{ill|Numata Interchange|ja|沼田インターチェンジ (群馬県)|lt=Numata Interchange|lt_ja=沼田インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Interchange|1=Numata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11142871}}, take {{ill|Japan National Route 120|en|Japan National Route 120|WD=Q5362009|lt=National Route 120|1=Japan National Route 120}} for approximately 30 minutes. == References ==
